// Anti-pattern: Route setup scattered across unrelated packages without a dedicated router package.

// --- File: cmd/server/main.go ---
package main

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

func main() {
	r := gin.Default()

	// Routes defined directly in main.go
	r.GET("/health", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"status": "ok"})
	})
	r.GET("/api/health", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"status": "ok"})
	})

	r.Run(":8080")
}

// --- File: handlers/user_handler.go ---
package handlers

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

// Anti-pattern: routes registered inside a handler package, not a router package
func RegisterUserRoutes(r *gin.Engine) {
	r.GET("/api/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"users": []string{}})
	})
	r.POST("/api/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusCreated, gin.H{"id": 1})
	})
	r.GET("/api/users/:id", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"id": c.Param("id")})
	})
}

// --- File: middleware/auth.go ---
package middleware

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

// Anti-pattern: even middleware file registers routes
func RegisterAuthRoutes(r *gin.Engine) {
	r.POST("/auth/login", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"token": "fake-token"})
	})
	r.POST("/auth/logout", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"message": "logged out"})
	})
}

// --- File: admin/admin.go ---
package admin

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"net/http"
)

// Anti-pattern: admin package also registers its own routes
func RegisterAdminRoutes(r *gin.Engine) {
	r.GET("/admin/dashboard", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"dashboard": true})
	})
	r.GET("/admin/users", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"users": []string{}})
	})
}
